title: An original resolution authorizing expenditures by the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.
plain_summary: This bill gives the Senate Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry permission to spend money and hire workers for two years. The time period runs from March 2025 through February 2027. The committee will get different amounts of money during different parts of this time. The biggest budget will be over seven million dollars in fiscal year 2026.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration. (text: CR S671)
subjects: Senate Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

sections.title: Section 1 General Authority
sections.text: The Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry has permission to do its job from March first two thousand twenty five through February twenty eighth two thousand twenty seven. This committee can hold hearings, write reports about those hearings, and do investigations as allowed by Senate rules. During this time period, the committee is allowed to spend money from the Senate's backup fund, hire staff members, and borrow workers from government agencies if those agencies and the Committee on Rules and Administration agree first. The borrowed workers might be paid back by the committee or work for free.

sections.title: Section 2 Expenses
sections.text: The committee has spending limits for three different time periods. For March first two thousand twenty five through September thirtieth two thousand twenty five, the committee cannot spend more than four million four hundred sixty four thousand nine hundred thirty five dollars. Up to two hundred thousand dollars of this money can be used to hire outside experts or consulting companies. Up to forty thousand dollars can be used to train the committee's professional staff members. For October first two thousand twenty five through September thirtieth two thousand twenty six, the committee cannot spend more than seven million six hundred fifty four thousand one hundred seventy four dollars. The same limits apply for hiring consultants and training staff. For October first two thousand twenty six through February twenty eighth two thousand twenty seven, the committee cannot spend more than three million one hundred eighty nine thousand two hundred thirty nine dollars. Again, the same limits apply for consultants and training.

sections.title: Section 3 Expenses and Agency Contributions
sections.text: The committee will pay its expenses using money from the Senate's backup fund. The committee chairman must approve written requests for payments called vouchers before money can be spent. However, some expenses do not need these written approval forms. These include paying regular salaries to full time employees, paying for phone and internet services from the Senate's security office, buying office supplies through the official supply keeper, paying the Senate post office, paying for copying machine costs from the security office, paying for recording and photo services, and paying for official mail costs through the security office. The Senate also has a separate account called Expenses of Inquiries and Investigations that will pay for employee benefits and other costs related to paying committee workers during all three time periods mentioned earlier.
